criterion: all four valley-partner conventions are present
interpretation: The response sensitivity test covers the working convention and three alternatives.

criterion: key-point response sensitivity covers the planned axes
interpretation: The test samples production grids and shell cutoffs without becoming a dense rerun.

criterion: the working tr_sewn reference remains positive
interpretation: The sensitivity study reproduces the production-scope positive sign.

criterion: at least one alternative partner convention changes the fixed-Frobenius sign
interpretation: The revision must not present the response as valley-sewing independent.
The revision can discuss valley-sewing sensitivity quantitatively rather than as a vague caveat.
The response sensitivity table includes evidence for how far raw partners are from the TR target.

criterion: working tr_sewn particle-hole spectrum error remains below 1e-8
interpretation: The production convention remains numerically PH-consistent.

criterion: alternative partner conventions show measurable PH-spectrum sensitivity
interpretation: Alternative partners are diagnostic stress tests rather than replacement production conventions.
